source.update_note: 2026-27 migratory bird data from AZGFD. Arizona has split dove seasons, band-tailed pigeon, sandhill crane, and Pacific Flyway waterfowl.

species.name: Dove (Mourning & White-winged)
species.season_raw: Early: Sep 1-15; Late: Nov 21 - Jan 4 (split season)

species.name: Goose
species.season_raw: Same zone dates as duck. Light goose season extends through Feb/Mar
species.bag_limit: 30/day max (20 white, 10 dark; ≤6 white-fronted, ≤3 large Canada)

licenses.name: Arizona Migratory Bird Stamp
licenses.covers: Required for all migratory bird hunting (dove, duck, goose, sandhill crane)

licenses.name: Sandhill Crane Tag
licenses.covers: Required for sandhill crane hunting (draw, 3-tag max)

key_notes.title: Arizona dove season is split: Early Sep 1-15 and Late Nov 21 - Jan 4. The Arizona Migratory Bird Stamp ($5) is required for all migratory bird hunting.

sources.label: AZGFD — Dove Seasons
sources.evidence: Dove: Early Sep 1-15; Late Nov 21 - Jan 4. Migratory Bird Stamp: $5.

key_notes.title: Sandhill crane hunting is by draw permit with 3-day hunting periods from Nov 21 through Jan 26. Up to 3 tags per hunter.

sources.label: AZGFD — Sandhill Crane
sources.evidence: Sandhill Crane: 3-day permit periods Nov 21 - Jan 26. $43 res / $45 nonres.

key_notes.title: Arizona waterfowl follows the Pacific Flyway with a 7-duck daily bag (higher than the 6-duck standard in most states). Duck season is split: Oct 17-25 & Oct 28 - Jan 31.

sources.label: AZGFD — Waterfowl Seasons
sources.evidence: Duck: Oct 17-25 & Oct 28 - Jan 31. 7 ducks daily.

key_notes.title: The Arizona Youth Combo License ($5 for ages 10-17) includes the Migratory Bird Stamp, making it a great deal for young hunters.

sources.label: AZGFD — License Fees
sources.evidence: Youth Combo (10-17): $5. Includes migratory bird stamp.
